original_hypothesis.title: Native Token Deposit/Repay/Bid Flows Broken Due to WETH Approval Requirement After Wrap
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: wrapNativeTokenInWallet wraps ETH to WETH and transfers it to the user via IWETH.transferFrom(pool, user). Subsequently, executeDepositERC20/executeRepay/executeAuction calls erc20TransferInLiquidity which does safeTransferFrom(user, pool, amount), requiring the user to have WETH approval for the pool. Users depositing native ETH will not have pre-approved the pool for WETH, causing all native token deposit, repay, and bid transactions to revert.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The wrap-then-pull pattern creates a circular dependency: ETH is wrapped to WETH and sent to the user, then immediately pulled back. The pull requires WETH approval that a native ETH user wouldn't have. This breaks a core protocol feature across multiple entry points.

proof_of_signal.pattern: wrapNativeTokenInWallet transfers WETH to user via transferFrom(pool, user). Then erc20TransferInLiquidity in the caller does safeTransferFrom(user, pool, amount) requiring user WETH approval.

preconditions: User calls any native-token function (depositERC20, repay, auction, etc.) with NATIVE_TOKEN_ADDRESS
User has NOT pre-approved pool contract for WETH spending

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: WETH transferFrom(pool, user) followed by safeTransferFrom(user, pool) without user approval
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: In WETH9, transferFrom checks allowance when src != msg.sender. The pool calling safeTransferFrom(user, pool, amount) requires user's WETH allowance for the pool.
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No self-approval mechanism, no use of WETH.transfer() instead of transferFrom()

analysis_questions: Does the specific WETH implementation on target chains skip allowance for self-transfers?
Are there any pre-deployment WETH approval steps in the deployment scripts?
Can users work around this by pre-approving WETH?
validation_requirements: Test native ETH deposit on target WETH implementation
Verify revert occurs at safeTransferFrom step
Check all native token code paths affected

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Not an attack per se but a complete DoS of native token functionality. Any user attempting to deposit ETH, repay with ETH, or bid with ETH will have their transaction revert.
prerequisites: User interacts with NATIVE_TOKEN_ADDRESS without prior WETH approval

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Complete inability to use native ETH for deposits, repayments, bids, and liquidations
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All users attempting native ETH operations
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Users can manually wrap and approve WETH as workaround

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No integration test for native ETH deposit/repay flows with actual WETH contract

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Immediately detectable on first native token operation

validation_result.impact_summary: All native ETH deposit, repay, auction bid, redeem, and liquidation flows revert because wrapNativeTokenInWallet sends WETH to the user, then the protocol attempts safeTransferFrom(user, pool) which requires a WETH approval the user never granted.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The wrapNativeTokenInWallet function at VaultLogic.sol:464-470 deposits ETH into the WETH contract (WETH minted to the pool), then calls IWETH.transferFrom(address(this), user, amount) to send WETH to the user. This succeeds because in WETH9, when src == msg.sender, the allowance check is skipped. However, immediately after, the calling function (e.g., executeDepositERC20) invokes erc20TransferInLiquidity which calls IERC20.safeTransferFrom(user, pool, amount). Since the WETH contract now sees msg.sender=pool and src=user, it checks allowance[user][pool]. A user who sent native ETH has no reason to have pre-approved the pool for WETH, so this call reverts. This affects every native-token-inbound code path: depositERC20, crossRepayERC20, crossLiquidateERC20, crossLiquidateERC721, isolateRepay, isolateAuction, isolateRedeem, and isolateLiquidate.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: Direct code evidence confirms the bug. VaultLogic.wrapNativeTokenInWallet (line 467) transfers WETH to the user. VaultLogic.erc20TransferInLiquidity (line 391) calls safeTransferFrom(user, pool, amount) requiring WETH approval. The pattern is: ETH→WETH at pool→transfer WETH to user→pull WETH back from user. The pull step fails without approval.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: After WETH.deposit(), the WETH sits at address(this) (the pool). transferFrom(pool, user, amount) sends it to the user. The user now holds WETH but has NOT approved the pool to pull it back. Any subsequent safeTransferFrom(user, pool, amount) will revert.
Called with from=msgSender (user) after wrapNativeTokenInWallet. safeTransferFrom(user, pool, amount) on WETH requires user's approval for the pool, which a native ETH user never granted.
Wraps ETH to WETH and sends to user, then calls executeDepositERC20 which tries to pull WETH from user. The pull will fail without user WETH approval for the pool.

calling_sequence: 1. User calls Proxy with depositERC20(poolId, NATIVE_TOKEN_ADDRESS, amount, onBehalf) + sends ETH
2. Proxy forwards to PoolManager.dispatch(), which delegatecalls to BVault module
3. BVault.depositERC20: detects NATIVE_TOKEN_ADDRESS, calls VaultLogic.wrapNativeTokenInWallet(weth, msgSender, msg.value)
4. wrapNativeTokenInWallet: calls IWETH.deposit{value: amount}() → WETH minted to pool (address(this))
5. wrapNativeTokenInWallet: calls IWETH.transferFrom(pool, user, amount) → WETH sent to user (succeeds: src==msg.sender in WETH9)
9. WETH.transferFrom: src=user, msg.sender=pool → checks allowance[user][pool] → user has no approval → REVERT
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The circular pattern of wrap-send-pull creates an impossible state: WETH is sent to the user at step 5, but step 8 requires WETH approval that native ETH users would never have set. In standard WETH9, transferFrom checks allowance when src != msg.sender. Since the pool (msg.sender) is pulling from user (src), and user has 0 allowance for pool, the transaction reverts at step 9.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts address WETH approval. DeployPoolFull.s.sol sets wrappedNativeToken via addressProvider.setWrappedNativeToken(cfgWrappedNative) but does not set up any approval mechanism.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: This is not an attack but a functional DoS of all native ETH operations. Any user calling depositERC20, crossRepayERC20, crossLiquidateERC20, crossLiquidateERC721, isolateRepay, isolateAuction, isolateRedeem, or isolateLiquidate with NATIVE_TOKEN_ADDRESS will have their transaction revert. The only workaround is for the user to manually wrap ETH to WETH and approve the pool contract, then call the function with the WETH address directly—completely negating the purpose of native ETH support.
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH severity. Complete breakage of all native ETH deposit, repay, liquidation, and auction functionality across 8+ entry points. This affects all users who attempt to use native ETH with the protocol. While users can work around this by manually wrapping and approving WETH, this breaks the core user experience and protocol design intent. During time-critical operations like liquidations, this could cause failed liquidation attempts leading to bad debt accumulation.
recommended_mitigations: Do not send WETH to the user in wrapNativeTokenInWallet. Instead, keep the WETH at the pool (address(this)) and skip the transferFrom(pool, user) call entirely, since erc20TransferInLiquidity will be called immediately after with from=msgSender. Adjust erc20TransferInLiquidity to handle the case where funds are already at the pool.

Alternative: Change the pattern to have wrapNativeTokenInWallet approve the pool to spend the user's WETH before transferring to the user. But this is more complex and still requires the user to hold WETH.

Simplest fix: In the native token path, after IWETH.deposit(), don't transfer to user. Instead, adjust the calling code to skip the safeTransferFrom step (tokens are already at the pool). This could be done by having a separate erc20TransferInLiquidityNative function that only updates availableLiquidity and verifies the balance.
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy PoolManager with all modules and standard WETH9 as wrappedNativeToken. 2. Create a pool with WETH configured as an asset. 3. Call depositERC20(poolId, NATIVE_TOKEN_ADDRESS, 1 ether, user) with msg.value=1 ether through the proxy. 4. Observe that the transaction reverts at the safeTransferFrom(user, pool) step due to insufficient WETH allowance. 5. Repeat for crossRepayERC20, isolateAuction, etc. to confirm all native paths are broken.
uncertainties: The exact WETH implementation used on target chains is not provided in scope. If a non-standard WETH implementation were used that auto-approves or skips allowance checks in all cases, this bug would not manifest. However, standard WETH9 (used on Ethereum and most L2s) does require allowance checks when src != msg.sender.
The IWETH interface is not fully shown in the provided code. However, the standard WETH9 behavior is well-established.
additional_testing_needed: Integration test with actual WETH9 contract for all native token entry points
Verify which WETH implementation is deployed on target chains (Sepolia WETH at 0xfFf9976782d46CC05630D1f6eBAb18b2324d6B14 is standard WETH9)
Test the unwrapNativeTokenInWallet flow as well, which has the reverse issue: it calls transferFrom(user, pool) requiring the user to have WETH approval for withdraw/borrow native token paths
